rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Beneficiary And Real Party In Interest Michelle Bajjalieh To: To Provide Amended Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One); To Provide Amended Responses To Requests For Production (Set One), And Produce Documents; And To Pay Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Before the court is Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani's Motion for an Order Compelling Beneficiary and Real Party in Interest Michelle Bajjalieh to [sic]: 1) To Provide Amended Responses to Special Interrogatories (Set One); 2) To Provide Amended Responses to Requests for Production (Set One), and Produce Documents; and 3) To Pay Monetary Sanctions. Michelle Bajjalieh opposes the motion in its entirety and requests that monetary sanctions be awarded to her. Michelle Bajjalieh provided supplemental responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 12, 15, 21, 24, 33 and Request Nos. 5-6, 8-12, 21-22. The objections based on a nonparty's right of privacy and relevancy as to the contact information of nonparties has been resolved by the parties. Michelle Bajjalieh's procedural objections to Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani's motion are OVERRULED. The infirmities are excusable and, in any event, Objector has not demonstrated prejudice and no substantial prejudice is apparent on this record. Going forward the parties will be expected to satisfy all requirements for discovery motions, including good faith meet and confer attempts and code-compliant separate statements. Further waivers of procedural infirmities are not contemplated. The parties are further informed that going forward each discovery motion must be limited to a single discovery vehicle. In other words, a party may move to compel further answers to special interrogatories posed in set one. A party may not file a single motion to compel both further answers to special interrogatories posed in set one and responses to request for production set one. Such requests must be presented in two separate motions. Each motion would be governed by different rules, merits separate analysis and will be resolved by separate orders. Counsel or a party seeking to reserve a hearing date for a motion must give the exact name of the motion to the clerk at the time the reservation is sought. If counsel or the party file a motion that does not match name provided to the clerk at the time of the reservation, the court will likely strike the motion. Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani's request for additional responses to Special Interrogatories 12, 15, 21, 24, 27, and 31-33 is GRANTED. The issue is relevance. The burden is on the responding party to justify an objection or failure fully to answer the interrogatories. Coy v. Sup. Ct. (Wolcher) (1962) 58 Cal.2d 210, 220-221; Fairmont Ins. Co. V. Sup. Ct. (Stendell) (2000) 22 Cal.4th 245, 255. Michelle Bajjalieh has not met her burden.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For An Order Compelling Beneficiary And Real Party In Interest Michelle Bajjalieh To: To Provide Amended Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One); To Provide Amended Responses To Requests For Production (Set One), And Produce Documents; And To Pay Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [3 of 3] Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 16, 2020. Remote appearance is encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For An Order Compelling Beneficiary And Real Party In Interest Michelle Bajjalieh To: To Provide Amended Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One); To Provide Amended Responses To Requests For Production (Set One), And Produce Documents; And To Pay Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 3] Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani's request for additional production in response to Requests for Production 5, 6, 8-24 is GRANTED. The issue is relevance. The initial burden is on the moving party to show both: (1) relevance to the subject matter (e.g., how the information in the documents would tend to prove or disprove some issue in the case); and (2) specific facts justifying discovery (e.g., why such information is necessary for trial preparation or to prevent surprise at trial.) Glenfed Develop. Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (National Union Fire Ins. Co. of Pittsburgh, Penn.) (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 1113, 1117 (citations omitted); see also Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (Guess? Inc.) (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98. Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani's has shown that the information is relevant to the subject matter of the action and either admissible or "reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Thus, the burden shifts to the responding party to justify any objections made to document disclosure. Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (Guess? Inc.) (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98. The response must "identify with particularity" any document or thing falling within any category in the demand to which the objection is made, and set forth the specific ground for objection. CCP 2031.240(b). Michelle Bajjalieh's general objections are improper and, overall, she failed to meet her burden. Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani's request for additional responses to Special Interrogatories 25-33 and additional production for Requests for Production 10-24 and 27-28 is GRANTED. The issue is privilege. Michelle Bajjalieh may provide a further response by serving a privilege log that adequately identifies each document the responding party claims is privileged and the factual basis for the privilege claim under Catalina Island Yacht Club v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 116, 1127. Any documents not adequately identified as privileged must be produced. Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani has prevailed and sanctions are mandatory, unless the court finds that Michelle Bajjalieh acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. See CCP 2030.300(d) and 2031.310(h). The court finds that Michelle Bajjalieh did NOT act with substantial justification and circumstances do not make the imposition of sanction unjust. Michelle Bajjalieh shall pay to Joanne Rajaei-Tehran $2,820.00 within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Michelle Bajjalieh's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Bajjalieh's opposition to the motion was without merit; additionally, the court cannot find that Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani requested information outside the scope of possible discovery, failed to meet and confer in reasonably good faith, or otherwise abused the discovery process. See C.C.P. 2023.010(a), (c), (h), and (i), 2023.310(h), 2030.300(d), and 2031.310(h). Movant Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani, shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative.
